question_relevance_notes.linguistic: Judge asked about text/signs, squarely in linguistic domain.
question_relevance_notes.regulatory: Judge asked about specific road markings/signs, squarely in regulatory domain.
question_relevance_notes.meta: Judge asked about vehicle/pole specifics, squarely in meta domain.

response_update_notes.linguistic: Agent restated that text was illegible; did not add new visual evidence.
response_update_notes.regulatory: Agent restated general road marking rules; did not identify specific distinguishing features.
response_update_notes.meta: Agent restated architectural observations; did not identify specific brand/logos.

verdict.judge_question_strategy_notes: The judge targeted the correct agents (regulatory, meta, linguistic) to find distinguishing features. However, the questions were somewhat generic ('Are there any specific...') rather than targeting the specific evidence the agents had already flagged (e.g., asking regulatory about the specific double line style mentioned in their evidence).
verdict.judge_synthesis_notes: The judge correctly synthesized the strong signals from the meta and regulatory agents (architecture and road markings) to confirm Malaysia, effectively ruling out Thailand and Indonesia.
